steps.title: Identify the context clue
steps.content:
The narrator says the soul “left its body to lose itself in the wild unrestrained beauty” and “only left a trembling ______ of its existence within me.” The soul has nearly departed; what remains is barely detectable — a faint indication, not a full presence. The blank needs a word meaning a barely detectable sign or remnant.
  • Choice A — Trace: a faint mark or remnant; fits perfectly — only a trace of the soul remains. ✓
  • Choice B — Opinion: an expressed viewpoint; the narrator is not offering a view, but describing an emotional experience. ✗
  • Choice C — Dispute: a disagreement or argument; no conflict exists in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — Command: an order or directive; no command is given or received in this scene. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: “Trace” is the only choice that captures the meaning of a barely detectable remnant left behind by the departing soul. That is the correct answer.
